LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MORTGAGE ELECTRONIC REGISTRATION SYSTEMS, INC. DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT TOOK THE MATTER UNDER SUBMISSION. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998 - AFTER HEARING THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: THE COURT HAS DETERMINED THAT DEFENDANTS DO NOT NEED TO BRING THIS AS A MOTION TO RECONSIDER AND RULES AS FOLLOWS ON THE DEMURRER. DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER ON THE 5TH CAUSE OF ACTION (BREACH OF COVENANT OF GOOD FAITH & FAIR DEALING) IS OVERRULED. PLAINTIFF HAS SUFFICIENTLY PLED FACTS SHOWING A CONTRACTUAL RELATIONSHIP AND THE ELEMENTS OF THIS CAUSE OF ACTION. ALTHOUGH DEFENDANTS WERE NOT A PARTY WHEN THE CONTRACT WAS ORIGINALLY CREATED, THEY ARE STILL BOUND BY THE COVENANT OF GOOD FAITH AND FAIR DEALING SINCE THEY ARE NOW TRYING TO ENFORCE THE DEED OF TRUST AND PROMISSORY NOTE. DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO THE 6TH CAUSE OF ACTION (CIVIL CONSPIRACY) IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO PLEAD THE FORMATION AND OPERATION OF THE CONSPIRACY AS TO THESE DEFENDANTS AND THE SPECIFIC WRONGFUL ACTS DONE PURSUANT TO THE ALLEGED CONSPIRACY. BECAUSE THIS WAS THE SECOND OPPORTUNITY PROVIDED TO HAVE PLAINTIFFS DO SO AND THEY HAVE NOT, THE COURT DOES NOT THINK THAT FURTHER LEAVE WILL CHANGE THAT RESULT. DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO THE 7TH CAUSE OF ACTION (BP 17200 VIOLATION) IS OVERRULED. ON 4/13/11 THE COURT OVERRULED THE DEMURRER AS TO THIS CAUSE OF ACTION AND ORDERED THAT PLAINTIFF DISTINGUISH THE CONDUCT OF EACH DEFENDANT WHICH PLAINTIFF HAS NOW DONE. DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO THE 8TH, 9TH, 10TH, 11TH AND 12TH CAUSES OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND UNDER FIRREA AND D'OENCH DUHME DOCTRINE AS TO THE PORTION OF THESE CLAIMS INSOFAR AS THEY SEEK RELIEF FROM ACTS BY INDY MAC AND ONE WEST OR MERS AS INDY MAC'S SUCCESSOR FOR THOSE ACTS BY INDY MAC. IT IS OVERRULED AS TO RELIEF SOUGHT BASED OPON SPECIFIC ACTS OF MERS AND ONE WEST.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-26-2011 Continued To Oct-21-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-17-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT MORTGAGE ELECTRONIC REGISTRATION SYSTEMS, INC. DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT.
Appearances: Nicholas B. Waranoff of Allen Matkins (273.7483) for defendant and moving party; Ryan Sanders of the Law Office of Uy & Henrioulle (510.835.3730) appearing via Court Call for plaintiff and responding party. Argued and the court took the matter under submission. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998 --- After hearing the court ruled as follows: the Court has determined that Defendants do not need to bring this as a Motion to Reconsider and rules as follows on the Demurrer. Defendants' Demurrer on the 5th cause of action (Breach of Covenant of Good Faith & Fair Dealing) is overruled. Plaintiff has sufficiently pled facts showing a contractual relationship and the elements of this cause of action. Although Defendants were not a party when the contract was originally created, they are still bound by the covenant of good faith and fair dealing since they are now trying to enforce the deed of trust and promissory note.
Defendants' Demurrer to the 6th cause of action (Civil Conspiracy) is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff has failed to plead the formation and operation of the conspiracy as to these defendants and the specific wrongful acts done pursuant to the alleged conspiracy. Because this was the second opportunity provided to have Plaintiffs do so and they have not, the Court does not think that further leave will change that result. Defendants' Demurrer to the 7th cause of action (BP 17200 Violation) is overruled. On 4/13/11 the Court overruled the Demurrer as to this cause of action and ordered that Plaintiff distinguish the conduct of each Defendant which Plaintiff has now done. Defendants' Demurrer to the 8th, 9th, 10th, 11th and 12th causes of action is sustained without leave to amend under FIRREA and D'Oench Duhme Doctrine as to the portion of these claims insofar as they seek relief from acts by IndyMac and One West or MERS as Indy Mac's successor for those acts by Indy Mac. It is overruled as to relief sought based upon specific acts of MERS and One West. Parties notified of the ruling by telephone. Mr. Waranoff is directed to submit a proposed form of order.

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-17-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT ONEWEST BANK, FSB DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT.
Appearances: Nicholas B. Waranoff of Allen Matkins (273.7483) for defendant and moving party; Ryan Sanders of the Law Office of Uy & Henrioulle (510.835.3730) appearing via Court Call for plaintiff and responding party.
Argued and the court took the matter under submission. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998
---
After hearing the court ruled as follows: the Court has determined that Defendants do not need to bring this as a Motion to Reconsider and rules as follows on the Demurrer.
Defendants' Demurrer on the 5th cause of action (Breach of Covenant of Good Faith & Fair Dealing) is overruled. Plaintiff has sufficiently pled facts showing a contractual relationship and the elements of this cause of action. Although Defendants were not a party when the contract was originally created, they are still bound by the covenant of good faith and fair dealing since they are now trying to enforce the deed of trust and promissory note.
Defendants' Demurrer to the 6th cause of action (Civil Conspiracy) is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff has failed to plead the formation and operation of the conspiracy as to these defendants and the specific wrongful acts done pursuant to the alleged conspiracy. Because this was the second opportunity provided to have Plaintiffs do so and they have not, the Court does not think that further leave will change that result.
Defendants' Demurrer to the 7th cause of action (BP 17200 Violation) is overruled. On 4/13/11 the Court overruled the Demurrer as to this cause of action and ordered that Plaintiff distinguish the conduct of each Defendant which Plaintiff has now done.
Defendants' Demurrer to the 8th, 9th, 10th, 11th and 12th causes of action is sustained without leave to amend under FIRREA and D'Oench Duhme Doctrine as to the portion of these claims insofar as they seek relief from acts by IndyMac and One West or MERS as Indy Mac's successor for those acts by Indy Mac. It is overruled as to relief sought based upon specific acts of MERS and One West. Parties notified of the ruling by telephone. Mr. Waranoff is directed to submit a proposed form of order.

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-15-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS ONEWEST BANK, FSB, MORTGAGE ELECTRONIC REGISTRATION SYSTEM, INC'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Appearances: Ryan Sanders of Law Office of Uy & Henrioulle (510.835.3730) appearing telephonically for plaintiff and responding party; Nicholas B. Waranoff of Allen Matkins (273.7483) for defendants and moving parties. Argued and the court modified its ruling as follows: the 2nd, 12th and 15th causes of action are sustained with leave to amend. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend as to 10th cause of action; no reasonable probability that defects can be cured by amendment. Neither moving defendants were the originating lender or broker, or had any role in the origination. Onewest did not assume the liabilities of IMB per its agreement with the FDIC. Non-judicial foreclosure under a deed of trust does not qualify as a collection of debt governed by the Rosenthal Act. Sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to the following: 3rd, 6th and 7th causes of action to plead when alleged fraud was made and/or discovered, which defendants made representations or concealed information, and whether that person had the authority to speak on behalf of the corporate defendants. As to the 4th cause of action plaintiff must distinguish conduct of the individual defendants. In the 5th cause of action plaintiff must plead facts showing a promise that would support estoppel. For the 8th cause of action plaintiff must plead facts showing what benefit was conferred on defendants. As to the 9th cause of action plaintiff has stated a claim for quiet title as to I M B but not to the other defendants. Plaintiff must plead what adverse claims are made to title as to all defendants. The 11th and 13th causes of action must be pled with particularity. The 14th cause of action must state facts showing the mortgage was a type subject to the translation requirement. Order signed in open court. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
